mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes A35 AMG engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned A35 AMG (M260.920) engine typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only. Fitting adds £2,000–£3,500 depending on location and workshop rates. A replacement M260.920 engine costs on average £5,834 in good condition, with a new or reconditioned engine costing up to £7,779. Main dealer new engines start from £9,000–£15,000+ supply only. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes A35 AMG engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M260 engine has a mixed reputation. The most critical issue is cylinder head failure due to increased wear on exhaust valve seat rings and valve guides. Mercedes has issued an extended warranty in some regions covering the cylinder head to 15 years or 241,000 km. Timing chain tensioner failure, thermostat housing leaks, and camshaft adjuster solenoid failure are also documented. With proper maintenance, these engines can be reliable, but the cylinder head issue is a genuine concern.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes A35 AMG?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main known issues are: (1) Cylinder head failure — causing misfires, rough running, and loss of compression; covered by extended warranty in some regions, (2) Timing chain tensioner failure — causing cold-start rattle and risk of catastrophic engine damage, (3) Thermostat housing leaks — plastic housing cracks and leaks coolant, and (4) Camshaft adjuster solenoid failure — causing rough idle and reduced power.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes A35 AMG engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £15,000–£25,000 A35 AMG, a £5,000–£7,500 recon is 20–50% of the car's value. For a clean example with full service history, replacement is often worth it given the A35's strong retained values. However, if the car has other issues (transmission, electrical faults, accident damage), evaluate total repair costs before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes A35 AMG engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The A35 AMG uses the M260.920 engine code — a 2.0-litre turbocharged four-cylinder producing 306 hp (225 kW) and 400 Nm of torque. The engine code is stamped on the cylinder block. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Is the A35 AMG cylinder head issue covered by warranty?

acceptedAnswer.text: Mercedes has issued an extended warranty in some regions (ECR-M0002-22-010) covering the cylinder head on M260 engines to 15 years or 241,000 km. This affects 2019–2022 A-Class, CLA, GLA, and GLB vehicles. Check with your dealer whether your vehicle is covered. If not, cylinder head replacement can cost £4,000–£6,000.

eraMap.title: Mercedes A35 AMG — Which Engine Does Your Car Actually Have?

rows.eraNote: 2.0L turbo four-cylinder — 306 hp, 400 Nm. Cylinder head and timing chain tensioner failures are well-documented. Extended warranty available for cylinder head

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes A35 AMG Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an A35 AMG engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). Cylinder head replacement is a major repair requiring specialist attention.

cta.label: → Compare A35 AMG engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Cylinder Head Failure (The Defining M260 Fault)
problems.symptoms: Check engine light, rough engine running, misfires (especially on one cylinder), loss of compression, high out-of-spec emission values
problems.typicalMileage: 18,000–44,000 miles — can occur as early as 5,000 miles

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you experience rough running or a check engine light, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: This is the single most critical issue affecting the M260 engine. Mercedes has acknowledged increased wear on exhaust valve seat rings and valve guides, which can cause insufficiently sealed combustion chambers. A warranty extension has been issued in some regions (ECR-M0002-22-010) covering the cylinder head to 15 years or 241,000 km. Some owners have reported failures as early as 5,000–8,000 miles. If you experience rough running or misfires, have the cylinder head inspected immediately. A £4,000–£6,000 cylinder head replacement is dramatically cheaper than a £5,500–£10,500 engine replacement. Check with your dealer whether your vehicle is covered by the extended warranty.

failureLink.label: Read the full cylinder head failure guide → /failures/m260-cylinder-head-failure/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise from the engine on cold start for a few seconds, engine misfires, check engine light with timing-related fault codes
problems.typicalMileage: 40,000–80,000 miles — can occur with insufficient oil pressure or infrequent oil changes

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling on cold start, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: Timing chain tensioner wear is a known issue on the M260 engine. A worn tensioner gives the timing chain too much slack, leading to a slapping noise, especially on cold start. If the chain jumps timing, catastrophic engine failure can occur. Using the correct specification oil (e.g., MB 229.5) and adhering to strict 10,000 km (6,000 mile) service intervals is the best defence against premature timing chain system failure. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, get the tensioner inspected immediately.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain tensioner guide

problems.title: Thermostat Housing Leak & Cooling System Issues
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss, overheating, visible coolant residue around the thermostat housing, low coolant warning light
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant level drops

problems.recommendation: The M260 engine's thermostat housing is made of plastic and can crack, leading to coolant leaks and potential overheating. If you notice coolant loss or a low coolant warning, have the thermostat housing inspected immediately. Overheating can cause warped cylinder heads and exacerbate the existing cylinder head issues on these engines.

failureLink.label: Read the full thermostat housing leak guide → /failures/thermostat-housing-leak/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Camshaft Adjuster Solenoid Failure
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, reduced power, poor throttle response, check engine light
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)
problems.recommendation: Camshaft adjuster solenoid failure is a documented issue on the M260 engine. Solenoid wear or oil contamination can cause rough idle and reduced power. If you experience these symptoms, have the solenoids inspected. Catching this early prevents more serious valve train damage.

failureLink.label: Read the full camshaft adjuster solenoid guide → /failures/camshaft-adjuster-solenoid/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: A35 AMG — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: May be covered under extended warranty (15 years / 241,000 km)
Preventative — catches the fault before catastrophic failure
Piston-valve contact means full replacement required

buyingChecks.title: Buying This A35 AMG? Check These First
buyIf: * Vehicle covered by the M260 cylinder head extended warranty (2019–2022 models)
* Full service history with regular oil changes (maximum 10,000 km / 6,000 mile intervals)
* No cold-start rattling — this is a critical warning sign
* Remainder of manufacturer warranty or extended warranty in place
avoidIf: * No proof of cylinder head warranty coverage or service history
* Cold-start rattle — indicates timing chain tensioner wear
* Check engine light with misfire codes — cylinder head failure risk
* Poor service history — the M260 is unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Mercedes A35 AMG is a genuinely impressive hot hatch — 306 hp, 400 Nm, and 0-60 in 4.7 seconds. But the M260 engine has a well-documented Achilles' heel: cylinder head failure due to exhaust valve guide wear. Mercedes has acknowledged the issue and issued an extended warranty in some regions (15 years / 241,000 km). Timing chain tensioner failure and thermostat housing leaks are also documented concerns. Buy a well-maintained example with warranty coverage, or budget for potential cylinder head work. Without history? Proceed with caution — the repair bills can be substantial.

cta.label: → Compare A35 AMG engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes A35 AMG (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or slapping noise from the engine (timing chain tensioner warning)
* Cylinder Head Warranty — Check if the vehicle is covered by the M260 extended warranty (ECR-M0002-22-010)
* Service History — Including the M260.920 engine and confirmed oil change intervals (maximum 10,000 km)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Look for misfire or timing-related codes
* Coolant Level — Check for frequent top-ups; thermostat housing leaks are common
* Rough Running — Any misfires or rough idle? Cylinder head failure may be imminent
* Oil Leaks — Look for oil residue around the engine
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
